paracetamol.use: Pain and fever relief (same family as acetaminophen).
paracetamol.side_effects: Usually mild at normal doses; nausea or rash can occur.
paracetamol.safety_warnings: Do not exceed labeled doses; can harm the liver, especially with alcohol or existing liver disease.

acetaminophen.use: Relieves mild to moderate pain and reduces fever.
acetaminophen.side_effects: Usually well tolerated; rare allergic reactions.
acetaminophen.safety_warnings: Overdose can cause serious liver injury. Check all products for acetaminophen to avoid double dosing.

ibuprofen.side_effects: Stomach upset, heartburn, dizziness; may raise blood pressure in some people.
ibuprofen.safety_warnings: Use caution with stomach ulcers, kidney disease, or blood thinners. Avoid in late pregnancy unless a clinician says otherwise.

aspirin.use: Pain and fever relief; some adults use low doses for blood clot prevention per clinician advice.
aspirin.side_effects: Stomach irritation, bleeding risk, ringing in ears at high doses.
aspirin.safety_warnings: Not for children/teens with viral illness (Reye risk). Bleeding risk increases with other blood thinners.

metformin.use: Helps control blood sugar in type 2 diabetes.
metformin.side_effects: Nausea, diarrhea, stomach upset, especially when starting.
metformin.safety_warnings: Rarely linked to a serious acid buildup (lactic acidosis), more often with kidney problems; follow prescribed monitoring.

omeprazole.use: Reduces stomach acid for reflux, heartburn, and some ulcers.
omeprazole.side_effects: Headache, diarrhea, stomach pain; long-term use may affect nutrient absorption.
omeprazole.safety_warnings: Long-term PPI use should be reviewed with a clinician; may mask serious conditions if symptoms persist.

amoxicillin.use: Antibiotic used for several bacterial infections as prescribed.
amoxicillin.side_effects: Diarrhea, nausea, rash; allergic reactions in some people.
amoxicillin.safety_warnings: Finish the prescribed course unless your clinician advises otherwise. Seek urgent care for severe allergy (swelling, breathing trouble).

sertraline.use: Used to treat depression, anxiety disorders, panic disorder, and related mood conditions.
sertraline.side_effects: Nausea, headache, sleep changes, dizziness, dry mouth, and upset stomach.
sertraline.safety_warnings: Do not stop suddenly without medical guidance. May increase suicidal thoughts in some younger patients during early treatment.

fluoxetine.use: Used for depression, obsessive-compulsive disorder, panic disorder, and some anxiety conditions.
fluoxetine.side_effects: Nausea, insomnia, headache, sweating, and nervousness.
fluoxetine.safety_warnings: Monitor mood changes closely, especially at the start of treatment or dose changes.

escitalopram.use: Used to treat major depressive disorder and generalized anxiety disorder.
escitalopram.side_effects: Nausea, tiredness, sleep disturbance, and sexual side effects.
escitalopram.safety_warnings: Do not combine with MAO inhibitors. Seek urgent care for severe allergic reactions or worsening suicidal thoughts.

clonazepam.use: Used for panic disorder and seizure disorders in selected cases.
clonazepam.side_effects: Drowsiness, dizziness, coordination problems, and fatigue.
clonazepam.safety_warnings: Can cause dependence and withdrawal. Avoid alcohol and sedatives unless prescribed together.

propranolol.use: Used for blood pressure, heart symptoms, tremors, and sometimes physical anxiety symptoms.
propranolol.side_effects: Fatigue, cold hands, dizziness, and slow heart rate.
propranolol.safety_warnings: Use cautiously in asthma and diabetes. Do not stop abruptly.
